title: RESTful API Design Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of its resource-centered URL naming, standard HTTP methods usage, and the core principle of stateless client-server communication.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily design philosophy of 'REST' (Representational State Transfer)?
options: An architectural style where URLs represent 'Resources' (like users or products) that are manipulated using standard HTTP methods.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which HTTP method should be used in a professional REST API specifically to 'create' a new resource?
In a professional project, REST URLs should represent 'nouns' (resources) rather than 'verbs' (actions).
Which and/or what was the primarily principle of 'Statelessness' in REST?
options: Each request must contain all the information necessary for the server to process it.
The server stores all status in memory.

questions.question: Does following RESTful API Design help build truly Fullstack applications by providing a predictable and standardized way for clients to talk to your server?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of any high-quality production web development process.
